title: Intense Indian Sloe Berries Pakora
description: A pungent and velvety Indian dish featuring sloe berries, this pakora is a unique and adventurous treat. It is fodmap friendly and features a leaf wrap steam method.
instructions: Heat the ghee in a deep frying pan over medium heat.
In a large bowl, mix together the chickpea flour, rice flour, grated ginger, cumin seeds, coriander seeds, and salt.
Add the sloe berries to the flour mixture and stir until they are evenly coated.
Using a spoon, drop small balls of the berry mixture into the hot ghee and fry until they are golden brown.
Remove the pakora from the oil and place them on a paper towel lined plate to drain excess oil.
Wrap each pakora in a banana leaf and steam them for 5 minutes.
Serve the pakora hot with a side of chutney or raita.
To make the chutney, mix together plain yogurt, chopped cilantro, and a squeeze of lemon juice.
Garnish the pakora with additional cilantro and lemon wedges if desired.
